narrative.headline: 2022 Honda Pilot: collision-avoidance braking tops the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 122 complaints with NHTSA. Forward collision avoidance is the most reported area at 27%, followed by the electrical system (21%) and engine (17%). Many of these reports describe the same theme: the car braking on its own when nothing is ahead. No fires or deaths are reported. The brakes draw fewer complaints (15%) but the most injuries (2) of any area, so they are worth a close look on a test drive.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 33 reports (27%), tied to 2 crashes and 1 injury. Owners describe the automatic braking activating with no vehicle ahead. Test the driver-assist braking carefully.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 26 reports (21%), linked to 3 crashes and no injuries. Reports mention false brake warnings and unexpected hard braking on the highway.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 21 reports (17%), with 1 crash and no injuries. Some owners describe early transmission leaks on nearly new vehicles.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 18 reports (15%) but the most injuries of any area: 2 injuries plus 2 crashes. Owners report the car continuing to brake or losing power after the pedal is released. Test the brakes thoroughly.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 12 reports (10%), with no crashes, injuries, or fires. Mostly transmission-related complaints, including early leaks.
